key_themes: Q1 2026 earnings significantly beat expectations with record trading revenue
Strength in wealth management reaching all-time highs
Positive stock price reaction hitting new 52-week highs with analyst target raises
Expansion into digital assets via first major bank Bitcoin ETF launch
Optimistic execution of integrated business model in favorable markets
summary: Morgan Stanley reported outstanding first-quarter 2026 results, exceeding expectations with EPS of $3.43 versus ~$3 estimates and revenue of $20.58 billion, powered by record equities trading (up 25%, beating by nearly $1B) and all-time high wealth management revenue. Shares surged 4-5% post-earnings to new 52-week highs near $192-194, with analysts raising targets (average ~$200, highs to $230) reflecting confidence in the diversified model. The firm also launched its first spot Bitcoin ETF by a major U.S. bank, adding to positive momentum amid a bullish broader market outlook.
sources: 15+ web news articles from CNBC, Yahoo Finance, MarketBeat on Q1 2026 earnings beat and outlook, 15+ recent X posts discussing earnings, stock performance, Bitcoin fund launch, and analyst views
